Title: Submit a real-time 270 eligibility inquiry and receive an active 271 response
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration/Insurance Clerk; patient has an active coverage on file; payer connection configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient coverage tab | 2. Select primary payer coverage | 3. Click Check Eligibility | 4. System builds an X12 270 inquiry | 5. Submit and await 271 response
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic; Payer=Acme Health; MemberID=ACM12345; ServiceType=30
Expected_Result: A 271 response is received indicating active coverage with plan, group, copay, deductible and effective dates parsed and displayed; transaction logged in audit

Title: Eligibility inquiry returns inactive/terminated coverage (AAA reject)
Preconditions: Insurance clerk logged in; patient coverage with a terminated policy
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient coverage | 2. Click Check Eligibility | 3. Submit 270 for terminated member
Expected_Result: 271 returns benefit status inactive/terminated with effective end date; UI flags coverage as inactive and blocks claim submission against it

Title: Eligibility AAA error - subscriber/member not found
Preconditions: Insurance clerk logged in; payer connection active
Test_Steps: 1. Enter an invalid member ID | 2. Check Eligibility | 3. Submit 270
Expected_Result: 271/999 returns AAA error code 72 (invalid/missing subscriber ID); user sees actionable error and is prompted to correct member identifiers; no false active coverage shown

Title: Display deductible, out-of-pocket max and copay remaining from 271 benefits
Preconditions: Active 271 received with EB segments for service type 30 and 98
Test_Steps: 1. Open eligibility result | 2. Expand benefit details | 3. Review accumulators
Expected_Result: Benefit accumulators are parsed from EB segments and displayed with remaining amounts, in/out of network tiers, and time period qualifiers

Title: Eligibility check via FHIR CoverageEligibilityRequest/Response
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint exposed; Coverage and Patient resources exist
Test_Steps: 1. POST CoverageEligibilityRequest referencing Patient and Coverage | 2. Receive CoverageEligibilityResponse | 3. Validate insurance.item benefits
Expected_Result: Server returns CoverageEligibilityResponse with outcome=complete, insurance.inforce=true and item.benefit money amounts; resource validates against R4 profile

Title: Eligibility request timeout / payer endpoint unavailable
Preconditions: Payer endpoint configured but unreachable
Test_Steps: 1. Click Check Eligibility | 2. Payer endpoint does not respond within timeout
Expected_Result: System handles timeout gracefully, shows retry/queue option, logs failure, and does not mark coverage active or hang the UI

Title: Pairwise eligibility across payer x service-type x network
Preconditions: Multiple payers and service types configured
Test_Steps: 1. Run eligibility for each combination | 2. Validate benefit response mapping
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination returns correctly mapped benefit tier and copay/coinsurance values without cross-contamination of plan data

Title: Audit log captures who ran eligibility and PHI accessed
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Run an eligibility check | 2. Open audit log | 3. Filter by transaction type Eligibility
Expected_Result: Audit entry records user, timestamp, patient, payer, transaction type and outcome; member ID is masked per PHI policy in any UI surface

Title: Create a prior authorization request for a planned procedure
Preconditions: Insurance/Utilization clerk logged in; patient has active coverage; planned procedure order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Prior Auth worklist | 2. Click New Authorization | 3. Select patient, payer, procedure CPT and diagnosis | 4. Attach clinical notes | 5. Submit
Test_Data: Patient=Jane Synthetic; CPT=27447; ICD10=M17.11; Payer=Acme Health
Expected_Result: Prior auth is created in Pending state with a tracking number, linked to the order and coverage; submission logged

Title: Block service scheduling when required prior auth is not approved
Preconditions: Procedure requires prior auth per payer rule; auth is Pending
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to schedule/perform the procedure | 2. System checks auth status
Expected_Result: System warns/blocks scheduling stating prior authorization is required and not yet approved; override requires supervisor with reason captured

Title: Prior authorization state transitions Pending to Approved to Used
Preconditions: Prior auth exists in Pending
Test_Steps: 1. Payer returns approval | 2. Auth moves to Approved with units | 3. Render service consuming a unit | 4. Auth moves to Partially-Used then Used
Expected_Result: Auth transitions Pending->Approved->Partially-Used->Used; each transition is timestamped and unit decrements are accurate; no transition skips validation

Title: Prior authorization denied transition and resubmission
Preconditions: Prior auth in Pending
Test_Steps: 1. Payer returns denial with reason | 2. Auth moves to Denied | 3. Add additional documentation | 4. Resubmit as new request
Expected_Result: Auth moves to Denied; a new linked resubmission is created in Pending preserving history of the original; user cannot silently edit a denied auth to approved

Title: Authorization unit boundary - service exceeds approved units
Preconditions: Auth approved for exactly N units; N already consumed
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to render N+1 service against auth
Expected_Result: System blocks or flags that approved units are exhausted; the over-limit service is not auto-attached to the exhausted auth

Title: Authorization expiry boundary - date of service after auth validity window
Preconditions: Auth approved with validity 2026-01-01 to 2026-03-31
Test_Steps: 1. Render service on date outside validity | 2. Validate auth applicability
Expected_Result: System flags the auth as expired for the DOS and does not attach it; user prompted to obtain a new authorization

Title: Prior auth via FHIR CommunicationRequest / Claim preauthorization use
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint supports Claim with use=preauthorization
Test_Steps: 1. POST Claim resource with use=preauthorization | 2. Receive ClaimResponse with preAuthRef
Expected_Result: ClaimResponse returns outcome and preAuthRef that can be stored and later referenced on the billing claim

Title: Pairwise prior auth across service x payer x urgency
Preconditions: Multiple payers with differing auth rules
Test_Steps: 1. Submit auth for each combination | 2. Validate routing and SLA
Expected_Result: Each combination is routed to the correct queue with proper SLA; emergent requests bypass standard hold per rule; no mis-routing

Title: Build a professional claim (837P) from captured charges
Preconditions: Billing user logged in; finalized encounter with captured charges and active coverage
Test_Steps: 1. Open claims worklist | 2. Select patient encounter | 3. Click Create Claim | 4. System assembles claim header, subscriber, provider, diagnoses and service lines | 5. Save as Draft
Expected_Result: Claim is created in Draft with correct billing/rendering provider NPI, payer, subscriber, diagnosis pointers and service lines mapped from charges

Title: Build an institutional claim (837I) with revenue codes and DRG
Preconditions: Inpatient encounter with room/board and ancillary charges
Test_Steps: 1. Create institutional claim | 2. Map revenue codes, HCPCS, occurrence and value codes | 3. Assign DRG | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Institutional claim is built with type of bill, revenue/value/occurrence codes, statement covers period and DRG correctly populated

Title: Diagnosis pointer mapping - each service line references valid diagnosis
Preconditions: Claim with multiple diagnoses and service lines
Test_Steps: 1. Open claim service lines | 2. Verify diagnosis pointers | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Each service line points to a valid diagnosis index; no service line references a nonexistent pointer; primary diagnosis is first

Title: Claim total equals sum of service line charges (financial integrity)
Preconditions: Claim with multiple service lines
Test_Steps: 1. Sum service line charges | 2. Compare to claim total amount
Expected_Result: Claim total exactly equals sum of line charges with correct rounding to 2 decimals; mismatch is blocked from submission

Title: Build claim from FHIR Claim resource with supportingInfo and careTeam
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint accepts Claim resource
Test_Steps: 1. POST Claim with patient, provider, careTeam, diagnosis, item and supportingInfo | 2. Validate resource
Expected_Result: Claim resource is accepted, validates against R4, and round-trips with identifiers, careTeam roles and diagnosis sequence preserved

Title: Scrub claim - missing required NPI is flagged before submission
Preconditions: Draft claim missing rendering provider NPI
Test_Steps: 1. Run claim scrubber | 2. Review validation errors
Expected_Result: Scrubber returns a hard error that rendering provider NPI is required; claim cannot be submitted until corrected

Title: Scrub claim - invalid NPI check digit (Luhn) detected
Preconditions: Draft claim with malformed NPI
Test_Steps: 1. Enter NPI failing Luhn check | 2. Run scrubber
Expected_Result: Scrubber flags the NPI as invalid via Luhn/checksum validation and blocks submission

Title: Scrub claim - CPT/ICD code validity and effective dates
Preconditions: Draft claim with a deleted/invalid CPT and an ICD code not valid for DOS
Test_Steps: 1. Run scrubber | 2. Review code edits
Expected_Result: Scrubber flags invalid CPT and any code not effective for the date of service; submission blocked with line-level messages

Title: Scrub claim - NCCI / mutually exclusive code pair edit
Preconditions: Draft claim with code pair subject to NCCI bundling
Test_Steps: 1. Add procedure pair without modifier | 2. Run scrubber | 3. Add appropriate modifier | 4. Re-run
Expected_Result: Without modifier the bundled pair is flagged per NCCI edit; with a valid modifier the edit clears; inappropriate modifier use is still flagged

Title: Scrub claim - gender/age vs procedure conflict
Preconditions: Draft claim with procedure inconsistent with patient demographics
Test_Steps: 1. Build claim with obstetric CPT for male patient | 2. Run scrubber
Expected_Result: Scrubber flags gender-inappropriate procedure; an age-inappropriate code likewise flagged; submission blocked pending correction

Title: Submit a clean claim and receive 999/277CA acknowledgements
Preconditions: Clean draft claim passing all scrubber edits; clearinghouse connection active
Test_Steps: 1. Click Submit Claim | 2. System generates 837 and transmits | 3. Receive 999 then 277CA
Expected_Result: Claim status moves to Submitted; 999 confirms syntactic acceptance; 277CA confirms acceptance at payer; status timeline updated

Title: Claim rejected at clearinghouse (277CA reject) is surfaced
Preconditions: Submitted claim with a payer-level data issue
Test_Steps: 1. Submit claim | 2. Receive 277CA rejection
Expected_Result: Claim moves to Rejected with the 277CA reason; claim is editable for correction and resubmission; rejection logged

Title: Claim state transition lifecycle Draft to Paid
Preconditions: Draft claim ready to submit
Test_Steps: 1. Submit | 2. Acknowledged | 3. Adjudicated | 4. ERA posted | 5. Paid
Expected_Result: Claim transitions Draft->Submitted->Acknowledged->Adjudicated->Paid; backward transitions are restricted; each step timestamped and audited

Title: Prevent duplicate claim submission for same encounter
Preconditions: A claim already submitted for an encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to create/submit a second claim for the same encounter and services
Expected_Result: System detects potential duplicate and blocks or warns requiring explicit corrected-claim (frequency code 7) intent; no silent duplicate billing

Title: Submit corrected/replacement claim (frequency code 7) referencing original
Preconditions: Original claim paid; correction needed
Test_Steps: 1. Open original claim | 2. Create corrected claim | 3. Set frequency code 7 and original claim number | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Replacement claim carries frequency code 7 and references the original payer control number; original is marked replaced

Title: Void/cancel claim (frequency code 8)
Preconditions: Submitted claim to be voided
Test_Steps: 1. Open claim | 2. Initiate void | 3. Set frequency code 8 | 4. Submit void
Expected_Result: Void claim is generated with frequency 8 referencing original; original claim status reflects voided; financials reversed

Title: API submit claim and poll status via REST endpoint
Preconditions: API credentials provisioned; clean claim exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST claim submit endpoint | 2. Receive transaction id | 3. GET status endpoint
Expected_Result: Submit returns 202 with tracking id; status endpoint returns evolving statuses; unauthorized calls return 401 and are not processed

Title: Auto-post an 835 ERA matching claim by payer control number
Preconditions: Submitted claim adjudicated; 835 ERA file received
Test_Steps: 1. Import 835 file | 2. System matches by payer claim control number | 3. Auto-post payments and adjustments
Expected_Result: Payment and contractual adjustments post to the correct claim/line; claim moves to Paid; remaining patient responsibility calculated

Title: ERA posting balances - paid plus adjustments equals billed
Preconditions: 835 received for a claim line
Test_Steps: 1. Post ERA | 2. Validate line balancing
Expected_Result: Paid + contractual adjustments + patient responsibility equals billed amount exactly; an out-of-balance line is flagged and not silently posted

Title: CARC/RARC adjustment reason codes mapped on posting
Preconditions: 835 with multiple CAS segments
Test_Steps: 1. Post ERA | 2. Review adjustment reason mapping
Expected_Result: Each CAS adjustment maps to the correct CARC/RARC with group code (CO/PR/OA/PI) and routes to write-off or patient responsibility per rule

Title: Patient responsibility (PR) posting creates patient balance
Preconditions: 835 with PR adjustments (deductible, copay, coinsurance)
Test_Steps: 1. Post ERA with PR amounts | 2. Verify patient ledger
Test_Data: PR1=20 deductible; PR2=15 copay; PR3=10 coinsurance
Expected_Result: Patient responsibility amounts post to the patient ledger by category; statement-ready balance reflects only PR amounts not contractual write-offs

Title: Unmatched ERA - no claim found goes to suspense
Preconditions: 835 references a claim control number not in system
Test_Steps: 1. Import 835 | 2. Matching fails
Expected_Result: Unmatched payment is routed to a suspense/unposted queue for manual research rather than posted to a wrong claim; reconciliation totals preserved

Title: Reversal/recoupment in 835 (negative payment) handled
Preconditions: 835 contains a reversal of a previously paid claim
Test_Steps: 1. Import 835 with reversal | 2. Post
Expected_Result: Prior payment is reversed and claim balance reopened; recoupment is reflected without creating a phantom credit; audit trail intact

Title: 835 file with malformed segment count rejected
Preconditions: Corrupt 835 file
Test_Steps: 1. Import malformed 835 | 2. Validate parsing
Expected_Result: Parser rejects the file with a clear error citing the segment/control issue; no partial posting occurs; file quarantined

Title: Database integrity - posted payment links to claim, line and ERA
Preconditions: ERA posted successfully
Test_Steps: 1. Query payment record | 2. Verify FK links to claim, service line and ERA header
Expected_Result: Payment row references valid claim, service line and ERA identifiers; no orphaned payment; amounts immutable after post except via reversal

Title: Denied claim creates a work item in denial queue
Preconditions: 835 with a denied line (CARC indicating denial)
Test_Steps: 1. Post ERA with denial | 2. Open denial worklist
Expected_Result: A denial work item is created with reason, dollar amount, payer and aging; assigned to denial team queue

Title: Denial reason decision table routes to correct resolution path
Preconditions: Denials with varied CARC codes
Test_Steps: 1. Post denials with different CARCs | 2. Observe routing
Test_Data: CARC=16 missing info->correct&resubmit; CARC=18 duplicate->review; CARC=50 medical necessity->appeal; CARC=22 COB->bill other payer
Expected_Result: Each denial reason routes to the correct workflow (correct-and-resubmit, appeal, COB rebill, or write-off) per the configured decision table

Title: Rejection vs denial distinction maintained
Preconditions: One rejected claim (277CA) and one denied claim (835)
Test_Steps: 1. Review both work items | 2. Verify classification
Expected_Result: System distinguishes pre-adjudication rejection from post-adjudication denial; rejections are correctable/resubmittable, denials may require appeal

Title: Denial aging buckets and follow-up SLA
Preconditions: Multiple denials of varying ages
Test_Steps: 1. Open denial dashboard | 2. Review aging buckets
Expected_Result: Denials are bucketed by age; items approaching timely filing/appeal deadlines are highlighted; an item past appeal window is flagged separately

Title: Bulk reassign denial work items to a specialist
Preconditions: Denial queue with multiple unassigned items
Test_Steps: 1. Select multiple denials | 2. Bulk assign to user | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: All selected items reassign atomically; assignment change is audited; partial failure rolls back without leaving items half-assigned

Title: File a first-level appeal with supporting documentation
Preconditions: Denied claim eligible for appeal; clinical documentation available
Test_Steps: 1. Open denial | 2. Click Create Appeal | 3. Select appeal level 1 | 4. Attach records and appeal letter | 5. Submit
Expected_Result: Appeal is created in Submitted state with level, deadline, attachments and tracking; linked to the denied claim; submission audited

Title: Appeal deadline boundary - block filing past payer appeal window
Preconditions: Denial with appeal deadline; current date past deadline
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to file appeal after deadline
Expected_Result: System blocks or hard-warns that the appeal window has passed; if overridden, requires justification and flags low recovery likelihood

Title: Appeal state transitions through levels to resolution
Preconditions: Appeal in Submitted state
Test_Steps: 1. Payer upholds denial | 2. Escalate to level 2 | 3. Payer overturns | 4. Claim reprocessed
Expected_Result: Appeal transitions L1 Submitted->Upheld->L2 Submitted->Overturned; on overturn the claim reopens for payment; each transition audited with outcome

Title: Appeal outcome overturned triggers claim reprocessing and payment
Preconditions: Appeal overturned by payer
Test_Steps: 1. Record overturn | 2. System reopens claim | 3. Expect corrected ERA
Expected_Result: Overturned appeal reopens the claim, clears the denial, and the subsequent ERA posts payment; recovery is tracked against the appeal

Title: Appeal documentation access is PHI-restricted and audited
Preconditions: Appeal with attached PHI documents
Test_Steps: 1. Access appeal as authorized user | 2. Attempt access as unauthorized role
Expected_Result: Only authorized roles can view appeal PHI attachments; unauthorized access is denied and logged; all views audited

Title: Determine primary vs secondary payer order
Preconditions: Patient with two active coverages
Test_Steps: 1. Open coverage tab | 2. System applies COB order rules | 3. Verify ranking
Test_Data: Coverage1=Employer PPO; Coverage2=Spouse PPO; BirthdayRule applies
Expected_Result: Primary and secondary payers are ranked per COB rules (e.g., birthday rule for dependents, Medicare secondary payer logic); order is displayed and used for claim routing

Title: Submit secondary claim after primary adjudication with COB amounts
Preconditions: Primary 835 posted with paid and patient responsibility
Test_Steps: 1. Generate secondary claim | 2. Include primary paid amount and adjustments (COB loop) | 3. Submit to secondary
Expected_Result: Secondary 837 includes prior payer paid/adjustment COB segments; secondary adjudicates against remaining patient responsibility

Title: Secondary payment not exceeding patient responsibility (no overpayment)
Preconditions: Secondary adjudication where allowed exceeds remaining balance
Test_Steps: 1. Post secondary 835 | 2. Validate total reimbursement
Expected_Result: Combined primary+secondary payment does not exceed billed/allowed; any overpayment is prevented or flagged; patient balance correctly zeroed or adjusted

Title: Medicare Secondary Payer (MSP) questionnaire drives order
Preconditions: Patient potentially subject to MSP
Test_Steps: 1. Complete MSP questionnaire | 2. System determines Medicare primary/secondary
Test_Data: Scenario={Working aged with EGHP, ESRD coordination period, Liability/Auto}
Expected_Result: MSP logic correctly determines whether Medicare is primary or secondary based on questionnaire answers per CMS rules

Title: COB conflict - both coverages claim primary is flagged
Preconditions: Two coverages both marked primary
Test_Steps: 1. Open coverages | 2. Attempt to set both as primary
Expected_Result: System prevents two active primary coverages or flags the conflict requiring resolution before claim submission

Title: Coverage modeled via FHIR Coverage with order and subscriber
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint exposes Coverage resources
Test_Steps: 1. GET patient Coverage resources | 2. Validate order, subscriber, payor, relationship
Expected_Result: Coverage resources expose order (rank), subscriber, beneficiary, payor and relationship correctly; secondary has order=2

Title: Apply payer-specific timely filing limit
Preconditions: Claims for multiple payers with different filing limits
Test_Steps: 1. Build claims with various DOS | 2. System checks timely filing per payer
Test_Data: Acme=90d; Beta=180d; Medicaid=365d; DOS vs Today
Expected_Result: Each claim is validated against its payer-specific timely filing limit; claims past the limit are flagged before submission with payer-correct thresholds

Title: Payer-specific required modifier and POS rules
Preconditions: Payers with differing modifier/place-of-service requirements
Test_Steps: 1. Build telehealth claim per payer | 2. Validate modifier and POS
Expected_Result: System applies the correct payer-specific POS and modifier combination; mismatched combo is flagged per decision table

Title: Payer claim format routing (837P vs 837I vs paper)
Preconditions: Payers with different accepted formats
Test_Steps: 1. Submit claims to each payer | 2. Observe format/route
Expected_Result: Each claim is routed in the payer-accepted format/channel; payers not supporting EDI generate a printable CMS-1500/UB-04 instead

Title: Contract fee schedule and expected reimbursement calculation
Preconditions: Payer contract fee schedules loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Build claim | 2. System computes expected allowed amount | 3. Compare on posting
Expected_Result: Expected reimbursement is computed from the correct payer contract fee schedule; underpayment vs contract is detectable when ERA posts

Title: Underpayment variance detection on posting
Preconditions: Claim with known contracted allowed amount; ERA pays less
Test_Steps: 1. Post ERA | 2. Compare paid+adj to expected allowed
Expected_Result: Variance below contracted allowed beyond tolerance is flagged as a potential underpayment and routed to follow-up/appeal

Title: Validate 837 X12 envelope ISA/GS/ST control segments
Preconditions: 837 file generated for submission
Test_Steps: 1. Generate 837 | 2. Inspect ISA/GS/ST/SE/GE/IEA segments
Expected_Result: Control numbers in ISA13/GS06/ST02 are unique and properly matched at SE/GE/IEA; delimiters and version (005010X222A2) are correct

Title: Reject inbound 999 with implementation errors mapped to claim
Preconditions: 999 returned with IK3/IK4 errors
Test_Steps: 1. Receive 999 | 2. Parse acknowledgement
Expected_Result: 999 functional acknowledgement errors are parsed and mapped back to the specific claim/segment so the user can correct the exact field

Title: Sensitive 837 transmission encrypted in transit and at rest
Preconditions: EDI transport configured (SFTP/AS2)
Test_Steps: 1. Submit 837 | 2. Inspect transport and storage
Expected_Result: PHI-bearing 837 is transmitted over an encrypted channel and stored encrypted; plaintext transport is refused; credentials not logged

Title: Add and verify a new payer with EDI payer ID
Preconditions: Admin with insurance setup permission
Test_Steps: 1. Open insurance/payer setup | 2. Add payer with name, payer ID, address and EDI config | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Payer is created with unique payer ID and EDI routing; available for coverage assignment; duplicate payer ID is rejected

Title: Assign coverage to patient with subscriber and relationship
Preconditions: Patient registered; payer exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient coverage | 2. Add coverage | 3. Enter member/group, subscriber, relationship, effective dates
Expected_Result: Coverage saved with subscriber, member/group IDs, relationship and effective/expiry dates; appears as active for eligible DOS

Title: Effective date boundary - coverage not active before effective date
Preconditions: Coverage with future effective date
Test_Steps: 1. Set coverage effective 2026-08-01 | 2. Attempt eligibility/claim for DOS 2026-07-15
Expected_Result: Coverage is treated as not yet active for the earlier DOS; eligibility/claim against it is blocked or flagged

Title: Member ID format validation per payer mask
Preconditions: Payer with a defined member ID format mask
Test_Steps: 1. Enter member ID violating payer mask | 2. Save coverage
Expected_Result: System rejects member IDs not matching the payer-specific format mask with a clear validation message

Title: Claim worklist grid is keyboard navigable with screen reader labels
Preconditions: Claims worklist loaded; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate grid using Tab/arrow keys | 2. Verify row/column announcements | 3. Operate filters and actions via keyboard
Expected_Result: All claim grid rows, status badges, filters and action buttons are reachable and operable by keyboard with meaningful accessible names and roles

Title: Denial reason and dollar amounts not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Denial dashboard loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Review denial status indicators | 2. Verify non-color cues
Expected_Result: Denial/approval status conveys meaning via text/icon in addition to color; color-blind users can distinguish states; contrast meets AA

Title: RBAC - only authorized roles can submit and adjust claims
Preconditions: Roles configured: BillingClerk (build), BillingManager (submit/adjust), Scheduler (none)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt claim submit as each role | 2. Attempt write-off as each role
Expected_Result: Only permitted roles can build, submit, or adjust/write-off claims; unauthorized attempts are denied and logged; least privilege enforced

Title: PHI in claims is masked in lists and exports per role
Preconditions: Claim data with member IDs and SSNs
Test_Steps: 1. View claim list as limited role | 2. Export claims report
Expected_Result: Sensitive identifiers are masked for limited roles in UI and exports; full export requires elevated permission and is audited

Title: Exploratory - concurrent ERA posting and manual adjustment race condition
Preconditions: Claim open for manual adjustment while ERA auto-post runs
Test_Steps: 1. Begin manual write-off on a claim | 2. Trigger ERA auto-post for same claim concurrently | 3. Observe final state
Expected_Result: System prevents double-posting/lost-update via locking or conflict detection; final balance is correct and consistent; no negative phantom balances

Title: Exploratory - claim with maximum service lines and special characters
Preconditions: Ability to add many service lines
Test_Steps: 1. Add maximum allowed service lines | 2. Include notes with special characters and long descriptions | 3. Build and scrub 837
Test_Data: Lines=50; Notes with quotes, ampersands, unicode
Expected_Result: System handles maximum lines and special characters without truncation, EDI escaping errors, or crashes; 837 remains valid

Title: Claim and payment financial reconciliation totals match ledger
Preconditions: Multiple claims and payments posted
Test_Steps: 1. Query sum of billed, paid, adjusted, patient-responsibility | 2. Compare to GL/ledger control totals
Expected_Result: Aggregate financial totals across claims/payments reconcile to ledger control totals; no rounding drift; reversals netted correctly
